theory.intro: A line graph plots points and joins them, usually to show how something changes over time. You read values by following the scales on the two axes.
facts: The **horizontal axis** usually shows time; the **vertical axis** shows the measured value.
Each gridline step is worth a fixed amount — read the scale before reading any point.
A line going **up** means the value is increasing; going **down** means decreasing; flat means no change.
steps: Read the scale on each axis to find what one interval is worth.
Go up from the time on the horizontal axis to the line.
Read straight across to the vertical axis for the value.
